generation.title: Slugify CLI Program
generation.summary: A stdlib-only Python CLI that converts input text into URL-safe slugs, packaged as a complete AINP program example.

brief.goal: Create a small, testable command-line program that turns text into lowercase ASCII slugs.
requirements: use only the Python standard library
accept text from arguments or stdin
include runnable unit tests and a README

audience.primary: developers evaluating AINP for software generation

risk_profile.intended_audience: developers and protocol implementers (advisory, not part of gate judgment)
risk_profile.rationale: Small local CLI example with no network, credential, personal-data or deployment automation behavior.

constraints.description: The generated program must be stdlib-only and deterministic for the same input.

enforced_by.limitations: Evidence records local tests and review only; it is not an independent security proof.

constraints.description: The example CLI must not perform network access, file writes, subprocess launches or credential handling.

enforced_by.limitations: Static review and tests cover this small example; they do not generalize to arbitrary generated programs.

constraints.description: The package must disclose that the program is AI-generated example content and that AINP validation is not a trust certificate.
Keep the implementation short enough to inspect in one screen.

acceptance_criteria.description: The CLI converts punctuation, spacing and non-ASCII accents into stable lowercase ASCII slugs.
The implementation is stdlib-only and has no network, file-write, subprocess or credential behavior.
The program README documents how to run, test and interpret the example's honesty boundary.

directories.purpose: Generated Python CLI source, README and tests
Generated unit tests for the CLI

files.summary: Program README with run commands, test command and honesty boundary

points.title: Run and test instructions
requirements: Show at least one argument-based CLI run command.
Show the unittest command for this example.

points.title: Honesty boundary
requirements: State that AINP evidence is not a security certification.

points.title: Slug normalization
requirements: Normalize accents to ASCII where possible.
Convert punctuation and whitespace to single hyphens.
Return untitled for empty slug results.

points.title: CLI input modes
requirements: Read stdin when no positional text is provided.

files.summary: Stdlib unittest coverage for normalization and CLI invocation

points.title: Behavior coverage
requirements: Cover normalization, fallback, max length, argument input and stdin input.
